scoring_notes: Each skill graded against 4 assertions derived from eval #1 (expected_output). PASS = assertion clearly satisfied with specific, actionable guidance present in the skill body. FAIL = key_miss — assertion not satisfied, generic, or guidance drifts from expected output.

skills.name: team-validation-dashboard
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: We have 5 projects across web, iOS, and backend. I need a dashboard showing validation posture so the team lead can spot which ones need urgent attention before next sprint planning. Set it up.
assertions: Names collect-team-metrics.sh as the first step and explains the projects.json registry (name + path entries)
Names team-dashboard.sh as the render step and describes color-coded terminal table output
Classifies projects into Critical (<60) / Attention (60–79) / Healthy (≥80) buckets with clear thresholds
Notes the data-availability caveat — coverage_pct / regression_count display as 0 until enriched benchmark format exists

old_skill.key_miss: None — snapshot already documented the 3-script workflow, bucket thresholds, and data availability note.

with_skill.key_miss: None — current version preserves all four pieces and adds a 'Why these thresholds' rationale (60 = CI gate floor, 80 = benchmark.min_aggregate match) which strengthens assertion 3.

skills.name: validate-audit-benchmarks
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I modified three hooks last week and want to verify I didn't introduce regressions before cutting the next release. Run the benchmark suite and compare against the last baseline.
assertions: Recommends running scripts/benchmark/test-hooks.sh (or aggregate-results.sh) as the entry command
Explains the 60/20/20 weighting rubric (hooks 60%, skills 20%, commands 20%) so the user knows what moves the score
Describes the baseline-comparison mechanism — results saved to audit-artifacts/benchmark-baseline.json, compare after changes
Shows how regressions are surfaced — e.g., a per-hook PASS/FAIL listing or delta vs prior baseline

old_skill.key_miss: Snapshot lists the scripts, weights, and baseline file but does not demonstrate the regression-surface output. Baseline comparison is described as 'review cat baseline.json' — user must interpret manually, no sample diff/per-hook verdict shown (assertion 4 FAIL).

with_skill.key_miss: None — current skill adds a 'Sample output' block showing per-hook PASS/FAIL verdicts, aggregate delta vs prior baseline ('prior: 98.1% — +1.9pp'), and explicit 'No regressions' line, satisfying assertion 4. Also adds a rationale paragraph for the 60/20/20 weights strengthening assertion 2.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I implemented the user login form, ran the e2e test, got PASS. Can I mark this task done?
assertions: Rejects the completion claim on 'I saw it pass' alone — requires personal examination of the evidence file
Demands the user open the screenshot and describe specifics (form fields, buttons, states) — not just that the file exists
Provides a structured checklist with read/view/examine/cite items the user can walk through
Maps each checklist item to good-vs-bad examples so the user can distinguish compliant from non-compliant descriptions

old_skill.key_miss: None — snapshot has the 5-point rule, 7-item checklist with good/bad pairs, and the rule that marking done is rejected without specific observations.

with_skill.key_miss: None — current skill preserves all four pieces and adds a 'Scope and relationship to gate-validation-discipline' disambiguation (the skill is the general form; gate-validation-discipline is for formal plan gates). Expanded description + 3 new triggers ('merge it', 'ready to close', 'checklist before marking complete') improve routing but do not change grading.

skills.name: visual-inspection
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I just redesigned the product landing page with a new hero section, feature cards, and dark mode toggle. How do I systematically inspect it visually before marking the redesign done?
assertions: Walks through all 7 inspection categories (Layout, Typography, Interactive, Hierarchy, Platform-Specific, Dark/Light Mode, Edge Cases)
Requires screenshots at minimum 3 breakpoints (375/768/1440px) for each mode
Covers dark/light mode explicitly — both must render with no invisible text / adaptive images
Produces a structured report at e2e-evidence/visual-inspection/report.md with findings classified by severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)

old_skill.key_miss: None — snapshot defines all 7 categories, the 375/768/1440px breakpoints, the dark/light mode section, the severity taxonomy, and the exact report path.

with_skill.key_miss: None — current skill identical on the four graded pillars. Improved description + 3 new triggers ('empty state check', 'loading state check', 'did I handle all states') enumerate the non-happy-path states the skill catches, improving discoverability but not assertion scores.

skills.name: web-testing
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: We're adding a new dashboard page that shows user data from three APIs, with interactive charts, a dark mode toggle, and user filtering. The team says 'it's just display code.' I need to define what layers of validation are mandatory.
assertions: Uses the decision matrix to classify 'new page/route' as YES for Layers 1–4 (rejects 'just display code')
Layer 1: test each of the 3 APIs with real curl — verify response structure, status code, fields
Layer 3: accessibility required (heading hierarchy, color contrast, keyboard nav) for the new page
Layer 4: Core Web Vitals (LCP / INP / CLS) with Good / Needs Work / Poor thresholds specified

old_skill.key_miss: None — snapshot has the decision matrix (New page/route row: YES YES YES YES -), the curl + jq Layer 1 recipe, the Layer 3 a11y block, and the Layer 4 metric table with all three thresholds.

with_skill.key_miss: None — current skill preserves all matrix content and adds a 'Why some intersections are skipped' section explaining each '-' in the matrix, which tightens assertion 1 (user now knows why Layer 5 is '-' for a pure display page). Description also distinguishes strategy skill from execution skill (web-validation).

skills.name: forge-benchmark
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I just ran /validate-benchmark on my React project and got a score of 68/100 (Grade: D). The breakdown is: Coverage 50, Evidence Quality 80, Enforcement 60, Speed 95. What does this mean? What should I fix first?
assertions: Interprets each dimension score against its formula (Coverage 50 = ≤2 journey subdirs; Enforcement 60 = ~3 of the 6 +20/+10 slots missing)
Prescribes a fix order by weighted impact — Coverage first (35% weight, largest gap), then Enforcement (25% weight)
Names specific fixes for Enforcement: install hooks, add rules, remove *.test.* / *.spec.* files from src/
Shows the math — adding journeys / enforcement slots moves aggregate back above 80 (B range)

old_skill.key_miss: Snapshot documents the 4 dimensions, tier formulas, and +20/+10 enforcement slots (assertions 1+3 PASS implicitly), plus the aggregate formula (assertion 4 PASS implicitly), but does not prescribe a fix-ordering framework — user must infer 'fix Coverage first because 35% weight × 50 gap = 17.5pp headroom' themselves. Assertion 2 FAIL.

with_skill.key_miss: None — current skill adds an 'Interpreting a drop in score' section with per-dimension triage playbook (Evidence dropped → check 0-byte files; Coverage dropped → journeys purged or new features missing; Enforcement dropped → which +20 slot zeroed out; Speed dropped → CI contention). This explicitly enables fix-ordering by telling the user which dimension to investigate first. Also adds a 'Sample end-to-end output' block showing the per-slot +20/+0 breakdown the user would see, satisfying assertion 2.
